Title: Effect of suppression of arabinoxylan synthetic genes in wheat endosperm on chain length of arabinoxylan and extract viscosity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan/xylan biosynthesis in wheat endosperm cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression or loss-of-function of TaGT43_2 can strongly reduce arabinoxylan amount, chain length, and flour extract viscosity, which may improve wheat for nonfood uses such as monogastric feed and alcohol production. In hexaploid wheat, deployment may require combining loss-of-function alleles across all three homoeologues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in starchy endosperm; RNAi effect assessed specifically in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Arabinoxylan is a major structural polysaccharide of endosperm cell walls and strongly determines aqueous extract viscosity relevant to wheat processing and feed/alcohol-use quality
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in extension/synthesis of the arabinoxylan xylan backbone, contributing to polymer amount and chain elongation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression of TaGT43_2 decreased total arabinoxylan in white flour. 2. TaGT43_2 RNAi reduced both water-extractable AX and alkaline-extractable AX amounts. 3. TaGT43_2 RNAi decreased intrinsic viscosity of WE-AX and AE-AX, indicating shorter AX chain length. 4. Flour extract relative viscosity was strongly reduced, with large effects attributable mainly to lower WE-AX amount and secondarily to reduced chain length.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Arabidopsis IRX9. Discussion interprets TaGT43_2 as a component of xylan synthase complexes rather than proving direct catalytic chemistry in this paper. No visible effect on plant growth, grain size, or grain appearance was observed under the endosperm-specific RNAi strategy.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Glycosyltransferase family 43 protein involved in xylan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression; HPSEC with in-line viscometry; monosaccharide analysis; relative viscosity measurement; comparison with null segregant controls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could require stacking loss-of-function across three homoeologues in hexaploid wheat; no visible growth or grain phenotype under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases arabinoxylan amount and chain length, leading to markedly reduced flour extract viscosity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important control point for AX amount and polymer size, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT43 glycosyltransferase family; orthologue of IRX9
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for synthesis of the target polymer

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan/xylan biosynthesis in wheat endosperm cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression or loss-of-function of TaGT47_2 can greatly reduce AX amount, chain length, and flour extract viscosity, offering utility for improving wheat for feed and alcohol-processing uses. In breeding, multiple homoeologue knockouts may be needed in hexaploid wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in starchy endosperm; RNAi effect assessed specifically in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Arabinoxylan is a major structural polysaccharide of endosperm cell walls and a major determinant of extract viscosity influencing processing quality
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in arabinoxylan/xylan backbone synthesis and chain extension; discussion suggests primary catalytic role within xylan synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression of TaGT47_2 decreased total arabinoxylan content in white flour. 2. TaGT47_2 RNAi strongly reduced both WE-AX and AE-AX fractions. 3. Intrinsic viscosity of WE-AX and AE-AX decreased, indicating shorter AX chains. 4. Relative viscosity of flour extracts fell strongly, with some lines showing about sixfold reduction of the variable component. 5. The paper supports TaGT47_2 as a key contributor to xylan chain synthesis in wheat endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Arabidopsis IRX10. Prior evidence cited in discussion indicates participation with a GT43 protein and an IRX14 orthologue in a xylan synthase complex in wheat. Discussion suggests IRX10 proteins are primarily responsible for catalysis within the complex.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Glycosyltransferase family 47 protein involved in xylan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression; HPSEC with in-line viscometry; monosaccharide analysis; relative viscosity measurement; comparison with null segregant controls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely requires combined loss of multiple homoeologues in breeding; no visible effect on growth, grain size, or visible grain phenotype under endosperm-specific RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly decreases arabinoxylan amount and chain length, thereby lowering flour extract viscosity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point and likely catalytic component of xylan synthase, but not explicitly proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT47 glycosyltransferase family; orthologue of IRX10
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for synthesis of the target polymer

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan substitution/decorative biosynthesis in wheat endosperm cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression of TaXAT1 lowers WE-AX amount and extract viscosity with smaller effects than TaGT43_2/TaGT47_2, offering an additional target to reduce viscosity while potentially causing less severe changes to insoluble AX.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in starchy endosperm; RNAi effect assessed specifically in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Arabinoxylan is a structural endosperm wall polysaccharide and key determinant of extract viscosity affecting processing properties
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers arabinofuranose to monosubstituted xylosyl residues of arabinoxylan, responsible for 3-linked arabinose monosubstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression of TaXAT1 decreased total AX mainly through reduction of WE-AX. 2. TaXAT1 RNAi caused a modest decrease in WE-AX intrinsic viscosity but no consistent effect on AE-AX intrinsic viscosity. 3. Long AX chains up to about 4000 Xyl still occurred, indicating less severe effect on maximal chain length than GT43_2 or GT47_2 suppression. 4. Relative viscosity decreased about twofold, mainly explained by reduced WE-AX amount and to a lesser extent lower chain length.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene mainly affects Araf 3-linked to monosubstituted xylosyl residues, yet RNAi also altered backbone-related polymer properties. Authors propose either participation in a complex making decorated oligosaccharides for AX assembly or indirect post-synthetic trimming of unsubstituted xylan regions.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Arabinoxylan xylosyl residues requiring 3-linked arabinofuranose substitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression; HPSEC with in-line viscometry; monosaccharide analysis; relative viscosity measurement; comparison with null segregant controls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely multiple homoeologues expressed in endosperm may need combined loss-of-function; no visible effect on growth, grain size, or visible grain phenotype under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces WE-AX abundance and modestly shortens WE-AX chains, lowering flour extract viscosity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for modification of the target polymer and influences its amount/properties
